title: Pecluma schkuhrii
taxonomy: Pecluma schkuhrii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pecluma, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. Pecluma schkuhrii is native to Central and South America, and it is often found growing on trees in tropical forests. The species is also known as the Schkuhr's pecluma, and it is classified as a vascular plant. Pecluma schkuhrii is a relatively small species of fern, with delicate fronds and a compact growth habit.

intro: Pecluma schkuhrii, also known as the Mexican tree fern or resurrection fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating cardiovascular diseases and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pecluma schkuhrii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pecluma schkuhrii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal supplements and remedies.
